defect: C2 serialization race: the racy subject engine (fixtures/engines/racy.mjs) awaits the per-id seeded variable-latency hook BEFORE the read-match-mutate critical section, with no lock around it. Under Promise.all the awaits race, so critical-section entry order != submission order and price-time priority is decided by the latency race. This is the spike's await-before-critical-section bug (SPIKE-FINDINGS.md, Arm 2), now resurrected as a runnable engine and proven RED by the live differential arm.
must_turn_red_gate: exchange-lob differential (whole-output trade-sequence diff vs submission-order serial replay)
how_the_gate_rejects: The C2 live arm (gate.sh --differential --engine fixtures/engines/racy.mjs --seeds 200) diverges at seed 1, trade index 0. `corrupted_trades` below is the racy engine's ACTUAL concurrent fill sequence captured from that seed-1 run; `correct_trades` is the reference serial replay (reference/matcher.mjs) over the same submission order. The fixture-replay gate (run.sh --input) diffs them tuple-by-tuple and REJECTS at the first divergence — the same first-divergence pinpoint the live arm reports.

live_provenance.note: corrupted_trades = engine.trades after Promise.all over the seed-1 latency hook; deterministic across runs (pure (seed,id) delay).

why_per_trade_checks_miss_it: Each corrupted trade is internally valid: I1 (price is a resting price), I2 (buyQty==sellQty), I3 (net-zero), C1 (no over-fill) all hold. Only the whole-sequence ORDER is scrambled — order 4 took id1@105 before id3 ever matched maker 2. Only the whole-output differential against the serial replay catches it, exactly the C2 lesson.
